use_when: A finite nonexistence or lower-bound claim has an exact CNF encoding that is too large for one solver run but can be partitioned by explicit, disjoint branching assumptions.
mechanism: Generate a deterministic, symmetry-normalized CNF; split it into a fixed prefix tree of assumption cubes; retain every cube, its status, and its parent relation as the resumable search archive. For SAT leaves, independently check the decoded witness. For UNSAT leaves, retain an LRAT/DRAT certificate and verify it independently. Accept a global nonexistence result only when a separately checked cover certificate proves that the retained cubes exhaust the normalized search space and every leaf has a valid refutation; prioritize further compute only on uncovered or uncertified cubes.
first_discriminator: On a toy instance with a known answer, create at most 16 cubes and require: each cube is satisfiable or has a checked proof, pairwise cube disjointness holds syntactically, and the CNF plus the negation of their disjunction is independently UNSAT.
experiment_template: erdos-835 -> hypothesis: a selected small Johnson-graph coloring instance has no k-coloring after fixing a canonical vertex/color normalization -> deterministic Johnson-CNF generator; split on the first normalized color variables; run a proof-logging SAT solver per cube and verify each LRAT/DRAT leaf with an independent checker (or Lean LRAT importer) -> decisive signal: either a decoded coloring passes an independent edge checker, or all cubes plus a checked cover proof establish UNSAT -> stop condition: stop after 64 cubes or when every generated cube has a verified SAT witness or UNSAT certificate; report only the covered prefix if any cube remains uncertified
failure_modes: The symmetry normalization is unsound and excludes valid colorings.
A cube cover has a gap or overlap, so refuted leaves do not establish the global claim.
Proof logs certify the emitted CNF but the generator-to-graph correspondence is wrong.
Certificate storage or checking cost exceeds the bounded-epoch budget, making the partition too fine.
change_rationale: This materially strengthens the existing encoding strategy: solver status is replaced by a resumable, coverage-accounted proof object. It changes selection by allocating work to uncertified cubes, preservation by retaining a complete frontier rather than only outcomes, and falsification by requiring independently checked leaf certificates plus an exhaustive-cover check.

use_when: Candidates belong to a finite, parameterized grammar and an exact verifier can return a small concrete input on which a candidate fails.
mechanism: Encode a bounded candidate grammar in incremental SMT. Begin with a small test set, synthesize a candidate satisfying every retained test, then run an independent exact verifier over the full finite domain. If it fails, add the lexicographically first counterexample to the permanent constraint corpus and repeat. Preserve a Pareto archive of candidates by coverage across independently chosen CRT residue fibres, rather than retaining only the latest candidate. Accept a witness only if a separately implemented checker validates full-domain coverage; accept a bounded negative result only if the final finite encoding is independently replayed for the stated grammar.
first_discriminator: On the standard distinct cover {0 mod 2, 0 mod 3, 1 mod 4, 5 mod 6, 7 mod 12}, require the loop to find a valid candidate and have an independent period-12 scanner accept it; then change one residue and require the scanner to return an uncovered residue that the next synthesis round excludes.
experiment_template: erdos-7 -> hypothesis: a distinct odd covering system exists whose moduli are distinct nonunit divisors of 315 -> python -m research.erdos7.cegis --lcm 315 --max-iterations 64, using incremental SMT for residue choices and an independent exact scan of residues 0..314 -> expected decisive signal: either a decoded odd system passes the independent 315-period checker, or a replayable UNSAT result rules out this explicitly bounded modulus grammar -> stop condition: stop after 64 verifier counterexamples, a checked witness, or a second-encoding-checked UNSAT result; otherwise publish the retained counterexample corpus and Pareto archive only
failure_modes: An UNSAT result is overclaimed beyond the fixed divisor-of-315 grammar or modulus bound.
A shared bug between the synthesis encoding and period checker falsely accepts a cover.
Residue or modulus symmetry normalization removes a valid candidate.
The counterexample corpus concentrates on one CRT fibre and causes search stagnation despite other informative failures.
change_rationale: This adds an active falsification loop absent from the current library: verifier-produced residues become persistent synthesis constraints and alter the next candidate selection. Unlike fixed SAT cube-and-conquer, it can cheaply expose and preserve the most informative failure cases before committing to a full encoding; unlike witness enumeration, every failed candidate produces a reusable exact exclusion.
